question: Dota 2: Team Liquid vs Nigma Galaxy (BO3)
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Team Liquid and Nigma Galaxy in the ESL One Birmingham: Western Europe Closed Qualifier Playoffs, scheduled for January 18 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Liquid" if Team Liquid win the match against Nigma Galaxy.

This market will resolve to "Nigma Galaxy" if Nigma Galaxy win the match against Team Liquid.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.liquipedia.net/dota2/Main_Page.

question: Series: Virtus.pro Kill Handicap (-17.5)
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Virtus.pro and  Pipsqueak+4 in the ESL One Birmingham: Western Europe Closed Qualifier Playoffs, scheduled for January 18 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Virtus.pro" if Virtus.pro secures 18 or more kills than  Pipsqueak+4 in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to " Pipsqueak+4".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Will "Devo - Devo" win Best Music Film at the 68th annual GRAMMY Awards?
description:
The GRAMMY Awards are presented annually by the Recording Academy. For the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards, nominations are scheduled for November 7, 2025, and the ceremony for February 1, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed music film that wins Best Music Film at the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards.

If, for any reason, no winner is declared by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or in case of a tie for the winner, this market will resolve in favor of the listed music film that comes first in alphabetical order.

The resolution source will be the television broadcast of the awards and the Grammy website (https://www.grammy.com/); however, other credible reporting may be used.

question: Jamal Shead: Assists Over 7.5
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for January 14 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jamal Shead records more than 7.5 assists during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Jamal Shead records 7.5 assists or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

question: Total Rounds Over/Under 45.5
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Vitality and Virtus.pro in the BLAST Bounty Qualifier, scheduled for January 14 at 1:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of rounds across all Matchs in this match is greater than or equal to 46.

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Will the total number of TSA passengers for January 21 be less than 1,600,000?
description:
This market will resolve to the total number of TSA passengers reported on January 21, 2026. 

If the reported total number of TSA passengers falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve as soon as throughput data becomes available for the listed date. Any revisions published to data for dates December 1, 2025 and onward prior to the release of data for all dates within the listed range will be considered.

If data is not available for the listed date by February 28, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the daily checkpoint throughputs as measured by the US Transportation Security Administration (TSA), https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes. Should this URL change or move locations, a new URL on the tsa.gov domain will remain valid to resolve this market.

question: Will "And The Adjacent Possible - OK Go" win Best Recording Package at the 68th annual GRAMMY Awards?
description:
The GRAMMY Awards are presented annually by the Recording Academy. For the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards, nominations are scheduled for November 7, 2025, and the ceremony for February 1, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed recording that wins Best Recording Package at the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards.

If, for any reason, no winner is declared by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or in case of a tie for the winner, this market will resolve in favor of the listed recording that comes first in alphabetical order.

The resolution source will be the television broadcast of the awards and the Grammy website (https://www.grammy.com/); however, other credible reporting may be used.

question: Spread: Wofford Terriers (-6.5)
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for January 14 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Wofford Terriers" if the Wofford Terriers win the game by 7 or more points.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Chattanooga Mocs". 

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

question: XRP Up or Down - January 15, 1:00PM-1:15PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the XRP price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the XRP/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/xrp-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream XRP/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

The GRAMMY Awards are presented annually by the Recording Academy. For the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards, nominations are scheduled for November 7, 2025, and the ceremony for February 1, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed producer that wins Producer Of The Year, Classical at the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards.

If, for any reason, no winner is declared by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or in case of a tie for the winner, this market will resolve in favor of the listed producer that comes first in alphabetical order.

The resolution source will be the television broadcast of the awards and the Grammy website (https://www.grammy.com/); however, other credible reporting may be used.

question: Spread: Utah (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for January 15 at 9:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Utah" if the Utah win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Stars".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Stars vs. Utah
events.description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for January 15 at 9:00PM ET:
If the Stars win, the market will resolve to "Stars".
If the Utah win, the market will resolve to "Utah".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

question: U.S. strike on Somalia by January 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a US-initiated drone, missile, or air strike on the soil of Somalia is announced or credibly reported to have occurred between market creation and the listed date ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including FPV and ATGM strikes as well as cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by any United States operatives, including military forces, intelligence agencies, or other U.S. government operatives, that physically impact ground territory within the listed country.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory (including rivers, lakes, ports, but excluding territorial sea) of the listed country counts.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land in the listed country’s territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, ground incursions, naval shelling, or cyberattacks will not qualify.

Any strike occurring during this market’s timeframe that is claimed by either Donald Trump or the U.S. government will qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

This market may remain open until the end of the second day after the resolution time. If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by that time, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

question: Will "Coleridge-Taylor: Toussaint L'Ouverture, Ballade Op. 4, Suites From 24 Negro Melodies - Michael Repper, National Philharmonic" win Best Orchestral Performance at the 68th annual GRAMMY Awards?
description:
The GRAMMY Awards are presented annually by the Recording Academy. For the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards, nominations are scheduled for November 7, 2025, and the ceremony for February 1, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed performance that wins Best Orchestral Performance at the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards.

If, for any reason, no winner is declared by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or in case of a tie for the winner, this market will resolve in favor of the listed performance that comes first in alphabetical order.

The resolution source will be the television broadcast of the awards and the Grammy website (https://www.grammy.com/); however, other credible reporting may be used.

The GRAMMY Awards are presented annually by the Recording Academy. For the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards, nominations are scheduled for November 7, 2025, and the ceremony for February 1, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed recording that wins Best Opera Recording at the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards.

If, for any reason, no winner is declared by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or in case of a tie for the winner, this market will resolve in favor of the listed recording that comes first in alphabetical order.

The resolution source will be the television broadcast of the awards and the Grammy website (https://www.grammy.com/); however, other credible reporting may be used.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - January 15, 1:45PM-2:00PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - January 16, 2PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will "People" be said 100+ times during the first episode of the Joe Rogan Experience this week? (January 25)
description:
The Joe Rogan Experience podcast releases episodes on https://www.youtube.com/@joerogan.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during the first released episode of the Joe Rogan Experience Podcast between January 19, 2026 and January 25, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old episodes or prerecorded clips are aired where people are speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will count toward this market's resolution.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

If no such episode of the Joe Rogan Experience Podcast is aired by January 25, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

JRE MMA Show episodes will not count for this market, only Joe Rogan Experience episodes.

The resolution source will be the released episode from (https://www.youtube.com/@joerogan).

question: Will there be at least 300 measles cases in the U.S. by January 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026, according to the CDC case counter by January 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter 'Total Cases' in 2026 (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

question: Map Handicap: BMB (-1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between BASEMENT BOYS and illwill in the CCT Europe Closed Qualifier: Series #13 Group D, scheduled for January 14 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "BASEMENT BOYS" if BASEMENT BOYS wins 2 or more maps than illwill in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "illwill".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

events.title: Counter-Strike: BASEMENT BOYS vs illwill (BO3) - CCT Europe Closed Qualifier: Series #13 Group D
events.description:
This market will resolve to "BASEMENT BOYS" if BASEMENT BOYS win against illwill in the named Counter-Strike match in the CCT Europe Closed Qualifier: Series #13 Group D, scheduled for January 14 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "illwill" if illwill win against BASEMENT BOYS in the Counter-Strike match.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

question: US mobile/internet outages caused by Iran?
description:
On January 14, 2026, widespread internet and mobile service outages affected customers of major U.S. telecommunications providers, including Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile, across multiple states. You can read more about that here: reuters.com/business/media-telecom/verizons-network-down-thousands-users-us-downdetector-shows-2026-01-14.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if it is confirmed, by the U.S. government, or a consensus of credible reporting, that the January 14, 2026, internet and mobile service outages were caused by, originating from, directed by, or officially attributed to Iran or any Iranian proxy by January 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Accusations from the U.S. government that are hedged or expressed with ambiguity (e.g., "We believe it was Iran") will qualify. However, purely speculative statements (e.g., "It could have been Iran") will not count.

This market will resolve once official statements from the U.S. government , or a consensus of credible reporting, have clearly confirmed whether the January 14, 2026, outages were caused by an Iranian cyberattack. If the cause of the outages has not been attributed to Iran by January 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the U.S. government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Will MattCS get exposed for cheating?
description:
This market will resolve to the CS2 content creator (primarily known for CS2/Counter-Strike 2) that HaiX (https://www.youtube.com/@HaiX) explicitly exposes for cheating (e.g., aim assistance, wallhacks, exploits, scripting, skin changer, or similar in CS2) in his upcoming youtube video.

If the video is released but exposes no qualifying CS2 content creator, or is not released by January 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If the exposed creator is not among the listed market options, the market will resolve to “Other”

If multiple qualifying CS2 content creators are exposed in the video, the market will resolve to the one whose name comes first alphabetically.

The resolution source will be the official video on HaiX's YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@HaiX) verified by title, description, content, and timestamps. Secondary sources include HaiX's X posts (https://x.com/KingHaiX) linking to or describing the video.

This market will resolve to the CS2 content creator (primarily known for CS2/Counter-Strike 2) that HaiX (https://www.youtube.com/@HaiX) explicitly exposes for cheating (e.g., aim assistance, wallhacks, exploits, scripting, skin changer, or similar in CS2) in his upcoming youtube video.

If the video is released but exposes no qualifying CS2 content creator, or is not released by January 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If the exposed creator is not among the listed market options, the market will resolve to “Other”

If multiple qualifying CS2 content creators are exposed in the video, the market will resolve to the one whose name comes first alphabetically.

The resolution source will be the official video on HaiX's YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@HaiX) verified by title, description, content, and timestamps. Secondary sources include HaiX's X posts (https://x.com/KingHaiX) linking to or describing the video.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - January 16, 3PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will Seattle have less than 3 inches of precipitation in January?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in inches in Seattle between January 1 and January 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).

The resolution source for this market will be NOAA, specifically the figure for January 2026 when the "Monthly summarized data" for "Seattle City Area" is selected with the variable set to "Precipitation" at the https://www.weather.gov/wrh/climate?wfo=sew link once that figure is finalized for the whole month of January 2026.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 2 decimal places (e.g., 1.54), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

question: Will Keir Starmer say "Mr. Speaker" 15+ times during the next Prime Minister's Questions?
description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

question: Series: Anyone's Legend Tower Handicap (-8.5)
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Invictus Gaming and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 15 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 9 or more towers than Invictus Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Invictus Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

events.title: LoL: Invictus Gaming vs Anyone's Legend (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win against Anyone's Legend in the named LoL match in the LPL Split 1 Group Ascend, scheduled for January 15 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win against Invictus Gaming in the LoL match.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

question: Will Russell Henley win the 2026 Sony Open in Hawaii tournament?
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Sony Open in Hawaii tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the 2026 Sony Open in Hawaii based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the 2026 Sony Open in Hawaii tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by January 31, 2025, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).
